title: [Bug] SQLite State Race Condition
body:
## Description
There is a potential race condition in the SQLite state validation logic. When multiple Podman processes start at the same time, they may simultaneously check for the existence of the DBConfig row and attempt to create it. This can lead to concurrent initialization attempts that are not fully serialized.

## Impact
If several Podman processes start concurrently (for example during system boot or when multiple container operations run in parallel), they may race during the initial database setup. In this scenario, one process may fail with an error if another process inserts the configuration row first.

## Current Mitigation
The current implementation uses a transaction with an exclusive lock. However, this does not completely eliminate the possibility of a race when multiple processes attempt initialization at the same time.

## Suggested Improvements
Use INSERT OR IGNORE or INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING to make the creation of the configuration row atomic and avoid failures when another process has already inserted it.

Alternatively, introduce a file-based lock before performing database initialization to ensure serialized access.

Document the expected behavior when multiple processes attempt the first initialization concurrently, clarifying any limitations or edge cases.

comments.body:
My gut feeling was that we were protected by the existing alive lock, but on further investigation we initialize the database before that lock is taken. Changing the existing `INSERT` to add `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` sounds like a perfectly reasonable solution (and much better than the performance hit we'd take with a file lock).

Interested in opening a PR on this?
Yes, I will open a PR about it
